_provenance.stage: Stage 1 — derived from live cards for retroactive Stage 2/3 verification
_provenance.note: This Stage 1 JSON was reconstructed from the currently-published cards so external Stage 2 (Gemini bias) and Stage 3 (Codex fact-check) reviews can verify what readers see. It is not the original analysis — the original pipeline was not run on this issue.

headline: Corruption Costs Malaysia RM47B Annually, 3% of GDP
context: Transparency International Malaysia estimated that corruption costs the Malaysian economy RM47 billion annually through procurement leakage, bribery, and regulatory rent-seeking, equivalent to 3% of GDP or the entire education development budget.

cards.text: RM47 billion lost to corruption annually: enough to give every B40 household RM15,600 in direct support per year
cards.sub: Malaysia's CPI score of 50/100 ranks 57th globally, worse than every advanced Asian economy

cards.h: Government procurement corruption accounts for RM28 billion, 19% of total RM150 billion annual government spending
cards.s: Average bribery cost for business licences is RM12,000, effectively a corruption tax that falls hardest on SMEs
cards.d: Government procurement corruption accounts for RM28 billion, 19% of total RM150 billion annual government spending Average bribery cost for business licences is RM12,000, effectively a corruption tax that falls hardest on SMEs

cards.h: 78% of Malaysians in 2025 Merdeka Center poll believe corruption has worsened or stayed same despite reform promises
cards.s: Public cynicism translates to lower tax compliance. Perceived corruption cuts voluntary tax payment rates by 12%.
cards.d: 78% of Malaysians in 2025 Merdeka Center poll believe corruption has worsened or stayed same despite reform promises Public cynicism translates to lower tax compliance. Perceived corruption cuts voluntary tax payment rates by 12%.

cards.h: MACC received 8,400 reports in 2025 but initiated prosecution on only 342 cases, a 4% prosecution rate
cards.s: Average time from MACC investigation to court verdict is 4.7 years. The accused hold office throughout proceedings.
cards.d: MACC received 8,400 reports in 2025 but initiated prosecution on only 342 cases, a 4% prosecution rate Average time from MACC investigation to court verdict is 4.7 years. The accused hold office throughout proceedings.

cards.text: Every hospital not built, every road not fixed, every school not equipped. Corruption doesn't steal money, it steals the future.
Like a household losing 3% of its income to theft every month. The family never sees the money, but the missing schools and clinics are visible everywhere.
cards.sub: Corruption costs are invisible in budgets but visible in unbuilt infrastructure and underfunded services

cards.text: Should Malaysia adopt a guilty-until-proven-innocent standard for unexplained wealth among all elected officials and senior civil servants?

confidence: Low — derived from published cards, original brief not on disk; submit for retroactive Stage 2/3 verification
